title: Richly Flavored Middle Eastern Lamb Tagine
description: A malty and glassy-jelly nostalgic Middle Eastern dish featuring molasses, this lamb tagine is a low-carb friendly option for dinner, cooked to perfection in a traditional tagine pot. The result is a richly flavored and aromatic meal.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides.
Add the molasses, ground cumin, ground coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add 1 cup of water to the pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro.
Traditionally, the tagine is cooked over a low flame, which allows the meat to cook slowly and the flavors to meld together.
